observations.note: CoT guidance block properly implements 4-factor prioritization with explicit XML thinking tags matching Anthropic format. Factors are quantifiable and independent.
Per-phase example loading correctly structured with offset/limit parameters in all three phases. Line ranges match documented boundaries (within ±5 line tolerance).
No accidental deletions detected. All existing requirements elicitation workflow logic and SKILL.md documentation preserved. Additions-only approach followed correctly.
Markdown formatting proper throughout. No broken syntax, consistent indentation, proper code block delimiters. Headers follow consistent hierarchy.
Per-phase progressive loading pattern demonstrates effective token optimization. Reduces Phase 1 context by ~219 lines (305 full - 86 phase 1 = 219 deferred).
Consider adding verification timestamps to line ranges in SKILL.md comments (e.g., 'Verified: 2026-02-19, ±5 line tolerance'). Aids future maintainers when examples.md is modified.

Story specification compliance excellent. All AC#1-AC#3 acceptance criteria met: CoT block with 4 factors + thinking tags + MoSCoW mapping; per-phase loading with offset/limit; phase boundaries verified.

Standards compliance verified. No conflicts with coding-standards.md, anti-patterns.md, tech-stack.md, or architecture-constraints.md. Markdown style consistent, no hardcoded secrets, no code duplication.

Test verification files (ac1-cot-guidance-verification.md, ac2-per-phase-loading-verification.md, ac3-phase-boundaries-verification.md) not created. These should be generated during TDD Phase 2 (Green).

Framework constraint TL-001 (maintenance coupling for per-phase line ranges) is well-documented in story notes. Consider extending examples.md with phase boundary markers to further reduce maintenance burden.
